title: Fix profile setup flow for pre-existing users

requirements.text: In the profile setup flow, before attempting to register a new user, check whether the authenticated user already has an existing profile in the backend. If a profile already exists, skip the ProfileSetupModal entirely and proceed directly to the main app experience without showing an error or doing nothing silently.

quotes: the latest draft asks the user to set up his/her profile, then does not react when the user is pre-existing
!User already exists, this should be avoided

acceptanceCriteria: When a pre-existing user logs in, the ProfileSetupModal is NOT shown.
The app detects the existing profile and proceeds directly to the dashboard.
No silent failure or unhandled '!User already exists' error occurs for pre-existing users.
New users (no existing profile) still see the ProfileSetupModal as before.

requirements.text: Ensure that only administrators can create a new profile for an existing user. Regular users must not be able to trigger a profile creation or re-registration if they already have a profile. The AdminPanel's ability to manage/create user profiles must remain unrestricted for admin users.

quotes: while the administrator is allowed to set up a new profile

acceptanceCriteria: Admin users can still create or update profiles for existing users via the AdminPanel.
Non-admin users cannot trigger a new profile registration if a profile already exists for their principal.
Attempting to register an already-existing user as a non-admin results in an appropriate user-facing message (e.g., 'You already have a profile') rather than a silent failure.

constraints: Do not alter the AdminPanel component or its conditional rendering logic.
Do not remove or modify the ProfileSetupModal for new users — it must still function correctly for users without an existing profile.
Preserve all existing features from Version 25.
nonGoals: Adding new profile fields or changing the registration form.
Any changes to Header, Footer, or Dashboard components.
